source: Sleeper player map (live) + NFLverse depth charts + User team context (authoritative)
note: Team context 2026 is authoritative for starters. Sleeper provides full roster. 2025 stats from NFLverse.

ARI.name: Arizona Cardinals
ARI.notes_2026: Love arrives as 1.03 pick — immediate RB1 candidate. Mike LaFleur hired as new HC (Shanahan & McVay tree). OC is Nathaniel Hackett. Arizona signed Tyler Allgeier from Atlanta, will compete with James Conner for Backup. Expect Trey Benson COULD be traded or compete for backup. Cardinals drafted Carson Beck Round 3 of 2026 draft & will compete with Brissett who is looking for a new contract. Michael Wilson MAY establish himself as a TRUE WR2 in 2026 (Trending in right direction). MHJ WR1 breakout candidate, but has yet to demonstrate consistency.

historical_2025.notes: MHJ showed flashes of being a true WR1. McBride locked in as top TE. Pass-heavy scheme when Kyler Murray was hurt and soft benched the rest of the season. James Conner & Trey Benson suffered season ending injuries. Michael Wilson Broke out with Jacoby Brissett at QB.

ATL.name: Atlanta Falcons
ATL.notes_2026: Falcons signed Tua Tagovailoa after release from Dolphins. Penix may be ready week 1 but possible he is no longer the starter. This will be determined in camp. New HC: Kevin Stefanski from Browns. Tommy Rees is new OC. Followed Stefanski from Cleveland. Bijan & London are core offensive players. Pitts wants long term exension, but was franchise tagged (could be traded). Branch round 3 — Georgia WR adds speed & could produce early. Brian Robinson Jr is a valuable handcuff for Bijan Robinson but shouldn't have stand alone value. London clear WR1, Zaccheaus returns in free agency. Jahan Dotson (former 1st round pick) added in free agency. Zachariah Branch could see large volume as a rookie if develops quickly. QB play may still hold team back.

historical_2025.notes: Penix takes over, offense increases, but Penix has some struggles. Penix tears ACL & Kirk Cousins becomes QB1. Young mobile QB could unlock London and scheme. Bijan elite. Pitts late season breakout earns franchise tag from Atlanta. Mooney very dissapointing.

BAL.name: Baltimore Ravens
BAL.notes_2026: Lamar expected to completely bounce back. Derrick Henry is the unicorn who never ages apparently (fall off coming but production still here as of now). Ravens draft Adam Randall RB in 5th round. Justice Hill will still operate as 3rd down back. Henry main back. Isaiah Likely left to Giants in free agency. Andrews signs extension. Durham Smythe TE & Matthew Hibner TE also drafted. Andrews should be TE1 but unsure what production will look like. Ravens picked up Flowers franchise tag and he is under contract through 2027. Lane could be a rookie breakout, opportunity is 100% there.

historical_2025.notes: Lamar injured most of season. Played through injury. Tyler Huntley struggled as back up. Derrick Henry declined still posted 1595 rushing yards despite brutal team performance. Flowers ascending. Andrews declining. Lamar still elite SF value. Likely struggled to produce with offense struggles.

BUF.name: Buffalo Bills
BUF.notes_2026: The Keon Coleman breakout may not ever happen. Bills traded for DJ Moore from Bears & under contract for 4 years. Large opportunity to emerge as main pass catcher in offense. Shakir reliable but no true ceiling yet. Allen 30 but still elite. Bills draft Skyler Bell WR from UConn 6 foor 192 LB 4.40 second 40 yard dash. Large opportunity for Bell in long run. Kincaid health remains a question. Opted to not get PCL surgery AGAIN & new HC Joe Brady says they will manage pain throughout 2026.

historical_2025.notes: Keon Coleman looks like a complete bust. Multiple Healthy scratches because he showed up late to team meetings. Shakir reliable slot. Cook great value & very underrated. Allen locked as SF1 for years. Shakir was only reliable reciever. Kincaid struggled to stay Healthy & Knox is not consistent. Serious lack of production from pass catchers. Mainly Josh Allen & James Cook show in Buffalo this season. James Cook landed contract extension this offseason.

CAR.name: Carolina Panthers
CAR.notes_2026: McMillan year 2 — primary target share expected. Brazzell round 3 deep threat. Young has 5th year option picked up. Trending in the right direction. Hubbard serviceable, but the former 2nd round pick Jonathon Brooks is coming back from second ACL tear(great potential but all dynasty managers know). it will be interesting to see how Chris Brazzell II works into offense but expect Jalen Coker to be WR2. Xavier Legette may be traded early in the season. Panthers are very frustrated with lack of production. Carolina also may be in the market for a free agent TE like David Njoku (or may potentially trade for one). .

historical_2025.notes: McMillan arrives 1.08 — immediate WR1 candidate. Bryce Young breakout, but not fantasy relevant yet. Panthers make playoffs. Tetairoa emerges as true WR1. Jalen Coker injured but when healthy is SOLID WR2. Xavier Legette is one of the worst 1st round WR of all time. Complete bust. Rico Dowdle overtook Chuba Hubbard as starter. Chuba had calf injury that he missed time for and played through. Chuba was incredibly inneficient. TE play is very rough. Sanders, Tremble, Evans all not providing any fantasy relevance.

CHI.name: Chicago Bears
CHI.notes_2026: Strong disagreement who the WR1 will be in 2026. Odunze looked fantastic early in 2025, but Burden stole the show in the back half of the season. Loveland has emerged as Williams favorite target. Caleb Williams is set to have an excellent season again. RB room looks to remain the same with Swift 1A & Monangai 1B. Bears drafted TE Sam Roush in the 3rd round. Cole Kmet may be traded. Highly relevant fantasty offense.

historical_2025.notes: Williams year 2 expected leap. Ben Johnson elite new HC. Odunze primary target. DJ Moore ate up targets but was not main Weapon. Luther Burden MASSIVE breakout back half of season. Coleston Loveland led the team in recieving & completely broke out in the second half of the season. Swift very productive but aging. Kmet basically irrelevant. Kyle Monangai emerged as highly relevant 1B RB to D'Andre Swift. Swift still posted fantastic numbers, but Monangai showed serious flashes as being consistent back.

CIN.name: Cincinnati Bengals
CIN.notes_2026: Burrow and the Bengals look to get back on track & healthy for the 2026 season. Chase & Higgins are set to have fantastic years if the team is healthy. Chase Brown should see his fantasy value stay high in 2026 with Burrow back but it remains to be seen if he is the long term RB solution for Cincinatti. TE play does not typically produce consistent fantasy relevance. Bengals have added heavily on defense this offseason & should be more competitive. Worth note, if the Bengals do not play well this season HC Zac Taylor is going to be fired.

historical_2025.notes: Chase locked in as top dynasty WR. Brown proving himself. TE position weak. Burrow turf Toe Injury missed almost entire season. Joe Flacco traded to Bengals by Browns. Chase, Tee, Brown fantasy value saved. Could not produce with AWFUL QB play from Jake Browning (literally worst QB in NFL History). Bengals offensive line struggled. Defense struggled even more. One of the worst defenses in the NFL & Bengals couldnt make the playoffs.

CLE.name: Cleveland Browns
CLE.notes_2026: Browns drafted OT Spencer Fano RD 1 & WR KC Concepcion RD 1 in 2026 draft. Round 2 they took Denzel Boston. Concepcion & Boston can have fantasy value IF Cleveland figures out QB position. Browns fired HC Stefanski & hired Jesse Minter. Jerry Jeudy will likely struggle to have real fantasy value with the new rookies introduced. Harold Fannin has strong value with David Njoku leaving Cleveland. Njoku is still a free agent & COULD potentially return to Cleveland but it is unlikely.

historical_2025.notes: Joe Flacco started the season as the starter afer Browns drafted Shedeur Sanders in the 5th round & Dillon Gabriel in the 3rd. Flacco eventually traded to Bengals. Gabriel started majority of the season and was AWFUL. Sheduer was decent when he played but also not fantasy relevant. Watson missed entire season recovering from torn Achilles. Only fantasy relevant player was Quinshon Judkins who missed the entire pre season due to legal issues. He eventually suffered brutal season ending injury. Dylan Sampson flashed but likely holds no value. Harold Fannin Jr. emerged as a fantastic NFL rookie TE with REAL upside.

DAL.name: Dallas Cowboys
DAL.notes_2026: Dallas has added significantly on defense this offseason and should be VERY competitive. Multiple games last year caused starters to be benched due to blow outs. Dak remains a highly productive agin QB with a well balanced pass and rush offense. Javonte Williams landed massive 3 year extension with Cowboys & George Pickens has been franchised tagged with no current plans to land long term extension. Ceedee Lamb looks to get back on track as a top 5 WR following a rough 2025. Pickens and Ceedee should both be viewed as Dynasty WR1s. Jake Ferguson is expected to also have a great 2026 season.

historical_2025.notes: Cowboys traded a 3rd round pick to the Steelers for George Pickens & he completely broke out. Ceedee Lamb missed time with injury & struggled with drops. Jevonte Williams was signed in free agency & was a top RB in the NFL (finally recovered from torn ACL, MCL, LCL, & PCL injury). Ryan Flournoy emerged as clear WR3 in Dallas offense.

DEN.name: Denver Broncos
DEN.notes_2026: Bo Nix will be 100% healthy by camp following foot injury in NFL playoffs. Broncos traded their 2026 1st round pick for Dolphins WR Jaylin Waddle. This will significantly reduce Sutton's role as the alpha in the offense. Pat Bryant & Troy Franklin will likely have trade interest but if they stay on the team will have reduced roles. Broncos backfield is incredibly difficult to project & will need to be monitored all summer. Denver extended JK Dobbins & Drafted Jonah Coleman in the 5th round. Dobbins expected to be 1A and Harvey 1B, with Coleman as reserve, but Sean Payton as HC can do whatever he wants. Broncos drafted TE Justin Joly in the 5th round who has a small chance to be relevant because Evan Engram is legit trash

historical_2025.notes: Broncos offense BROKE OUT in 2025. Bo Nix emerged as GREAT low end QB1 in fantasy. WR room was difficult to predict other than Sutton (Bo Nix favorite target). Troy Franklin broke out as the WR2 and was a great deep threat. Broncos drafted RJ Harvey high in the draft but then signed JK Dobbins in free agency. Dobbins functioned as the RB1 & Harvey worked in. Looked good when in, but when Dobbins went down & went to IR, Harvey was highly productive in the back half of the season. Evan Engram was a WALKING TRASH CAN and one of the biggest dissapointments in the entire NFL. Pat Bryant looked solid & so did Marvin Mims Jr as depth players.

DET.name: Detroit Lions
DET.notes_2026: Gibbs + Williams + ARSB all dynasty assets. David Montgomery was traded to the Houston Texans & opens the opportunity for Gibbs to be completely featured in this offense. If he can stay healthy, he may be the RB1 in fantasy for years to come. Lions brought in Isiah Pacheco as RB2 from the Chiefs on a 1 year deal. He is not expected to play very much, but could be relevant with Gibbs injury. Khalif Raymond heads to Chicago Bears & Kendrick Law COULD emerge as a solid WR4 or WR3 in the long run. Isaac TeSlaa holds significant value if St Brown or Williams were injured. Williams & St Brown are both excellent assets. Goff remains a interesting aging QB that is a low end QB1 in fantasy with a VERY high weekly ceiling but no mobility upside.

historical_2025.notes: Gibbs elite dynasty RB. Williams ascending WR2. ARSB still WR1. LaPorta consistent but suffered serious back injury that caused him to miss the half of the 2025 season. Montgomery took a back seat to Gibbs but was productive when he played. Offense struggled more than usual & missed the playoffs unexpectedly. Goff remains a low end QB1 with high upside because of the high powered oppportunity for Lions to drop 50 points in any given game. Raymond functioned as a gadget WR3 and held little value while TeSlaa showed serious flashes and was a red zone power house.

GB.name: Green Bay Packers
GB.notes_2026: Key departures: Romeo Doubs to New England Patriots, Wicks to Eagles. Massive opportunity for Watson, Golden, and Reed. We will have to see who plays the most on 2 WR sets. Reed landed 3 year 50m extension with packers. Golden could be primed for a breakout. Kraft should be recovered from torn ACL early in the season & Musgrave will fill in if he misses time but no true upside to him. Savion Williams will compete for WR4 but it is unlikely he holds any true value. Jordan Love needs to stay healthy but could be primed for a great 2026 season. Josh Jacobs is showing signs for concern with efficiency & managers need to think about him, however, packers have not brought in any competition so in the meantime he should be ok.

historical_2025.notes: Reed missed extended time with injury and only played 7 games. Doubs emerged as WR1 with Christian Watson showing flashes of being a true X/Deep threat WR at times. Tucker Kraft tore ACL & missed half of the season. Josh Jacobs regressed but so did the entire offense. Malik Willis played 4 games with Jordan Love missing time due to injury and BALLED OUT. Packers drafted Matthew Golden in the 1st round of the NFL draft & he did not get involved much on the offense. Golden looked good when he got the ball, but was not a massive part of the game plan

HOU.name: Houston Texans
HOU.notes_2026: Nico Collins remains a high producing dynasty WR when CJ Stroud plays well. Texans have to improve O line play for offense to provide large fantasy relevance. Traded for David Montgomery from the Lions & He will operate as RB1 with Woody Marks expected to also work in. CJ Stroud has struggled since his rookie season & is a mid range QB2 most weeks in fantasy. Texans do expect to extend him long term despite poor play. Jayden Higgins is an interesting year 2 breakout candidate but Tank Dell also returns in 2026 (We will see if his knee still works).

historical_2025.notes: Collins back healthy — WR1. Dell misses entire season due to 2024 knee injury. Joe Mixon misses entire season with foot injury (shot himself in the foot is the word on the street). Pass game struggled because Stroud regressed behind HORRENDOUS O line play. Drafted Higgins & Noel in 2nd & 3rd round. Higgins showed a lot of promise. Could progress. Nick Chubb regressed heavily (cooked). Woody Marks showed a lot of promise as a rookie RB. Strong recieving back. Not projected to be workhorse back.

IND.name: Indianapolis Colts
IND.notes_2026: Anthony Richardson will be traded or released. Daniel Jones on track for full recovery from torn achilles & landed extension. Very positive for offense. Jonathan Taylor aging but HIGHLY productive. Alec Pierce lands MASSVIVE 4 year contract & Colts invested in him as their WR1. Josh Downs is primed for an increase workload & could truly break out in 2026. Michael Pittman Jr. traded to Steelers. Tyler Warren is a top TE in dynasty & could have a MASSIVE season next year.

historical_2025.notes: Colts sign Daniel Jones who beats out Anthony Richardson in camp. Alec Pierce emerges as ELITE deep threat WR. Pittman Jr. productive but takes back seat behind Pierce. Tyler Warren drafted in the 1st round & makes immediate impact. Jonathan Taylor was on pace for historic season before Daniel Jones tears achilles. Replaced Jones with Philip Rivers (offense struggled). Josh Downs struggled with injuries but highly productive slot WR. DJ Giddens pure back up to Taylor. Taylor workhorse back. Mitchell traded to the Jets.

JAX.name: Jacksonville Jaguars
JAX.notes_2026: Travis Etienne Jr. has left the Jaguars in free agency for the Saints. Opening door for Bhayshul Tuten to operate as team lead back. Jaguars sign Chris Rodriguez Jr. to 2 year deal from Commanders. Jaguars draft Nate Boerkircher (TE) in 2nd round and should cause competition for Brenton Strange. Also drafted Josh Cameron & CJ Williams both WR in the 6th round. Travis Hunter is expected to play more CB than WR in 2026, but we will need confirmation through camp (very  bad for dynasty value if true). Brian Thomas Jr looks to return to his rookie form as a true WR1 but Jakobi Meyers may operate as the teams 1A. Parker Washington has potential to be massive contributor in 2026 if BTJ struggles continue.

historical_2025.notes: Jaguars trade future first to acquire Travis Hunter who has slow start to season. Posts 1 massive game & injured for rest of season. Jaguars trade for Jakobi Meyers from Raiders & extend 3 years. Operates as 1b WR. Brian Thomas Jr. has worst sophmore slump season to record & struggles with drop & production issues. Parker Washington leads team in recieving & has breakout from WR3 position. Travis Etienne Jr. has career turn around & has his best season of career. Brenton Strange struggled with injury but productive when healthy. Trevor Lawrence shows promise as QB but still not QB1 on weekly basis for fantasy, could become a QB1 though.

KC.name: Kansas City Chiefs
KC.notes_2026: Rashee Rice is slotted to operate as WR1 & holds high value for 2026 season. Mahomes should be recovered from torn ACL. Kelce is in his final season most likely. Xavier Worthy has struggled to produce, could break out, but not looking great right now. Chiefs sign Kenneth Walker III in free agency (reigning superbowl MVP RB from seahawks) and he is set for monster season. Brashard Smith should operate as receiving back and Emmett Johnson will battle for 3rd string back with Emari Demercado.

historical_2025.notes: Worthy struggled again to meet expectations. Rice returned following suspension & was highly productive. Hollywood Brown retruned from injury & was not consistent. Does not have long term value. Kelce looked awful but still produced due to volume but is washed at this point. Pacheco 1A & Hunt 1B. Both AWFUL RB & difficult to start in fantasy. Mahomes dynasty value declining with age & ACL tear end of season but still elite SF. Jalen Royals drafted & produced almost nothing but interesting prospect.

LAC.name: Los Angeles Chargers
LAC.notes_2026: Chargers bring in Mike McDaniel as offensvie coordinator who could SUPERCHARGE offense. Herbert ascending as top 7 dynasty QB. Keenan Allen remains a free agent. Ladd & QJ should operate as 1A & 1B with Ladd having more potential. Tre' Harris should operate as WR3 if no one else is brought in. TE Oronde Gadsden could be very good. RB room is complex. Hampton is the presumed starter week 1 with massive potential but Vidal has been very capable as a back up & chargers also signed Keaton Mitchell to 2 year deal in free agency. Question mark people should think about in usage. Thompson round 4.

historical_2025.notes: Omarion Hampton drafted 1st round. Many expected strong run game, but injuries to Slater & Alt on O line cause Chargers to throw WAY MORE than expected. Quentin Johnson BREAKOUT first half of season but faded off later. Keenan Allen returned & saw significant volume causing Ladd McConkey production to drop significantly. Ladd was dissapointing. Herbert produced well. Vidal was a great 1B RB & filled in with Hampton down. Hampton had a very dissapointing rookie season but showed promise. Tre' Harris dissapointing rookie season and underused. KeAndre Lambert-Smith gained pre season hype but did not produce at all. TE Oronde Gadsden broke out & will be the Chargers TE of the future.

LAR.name: Los Angeles Rams
LAR.notes_2026: Rams draft Ty Simpson in the 1st round but Stafford is rumored to be signing 2 year extension. Simpson likely to sit until 2028 season. Best for him for development. Stafford will play at MVP level until retires more than likely. Puka has had off field trouble and spent time in Rehab, but should be ready by week 1. Rams draft TE Max Klare early & will likely be TE1 ahead of Parkinson but may not be. RB room is confusing, Corum has potential to overtake Kyren Williams in 2026 season. Both backs hold high value if the other is hurt.

historical_2025.notes: Nacua is a dynasty cornerstone. Rams sign Davante Adams & he produces at high level when healthy (especially in red zone). Stafford aging but wins MVP. Blake Corum worked in & eroded Kyren Williams dynasty value. Much more of a split than many people thought it would be. Cooper Kupp left for Seahawks.

LV.name: Las Vegas Raiders
LV.notes_2026: Mendoza 1.01 — franchise QB of the future. Raiders also signed Kirk Cousins & he is assumed week 1 starter. Mendoza may not play early half of season unless beats Cousins out in camp. Raiders spent big in free agency and O line should be better but still bad. Buy Bowers long-term. Monitor Mendoza development. Raiders will probably sign a veteran WR who could operate as WR1, either Jauan Jennings or Diggs potentially. Tyreek Hill also an option. Mike Washington Jr will back up Ashton Jeanty. New HC Klint Kubiak former Seahawks OC should also boost offense creativity.

historical_2025.notes: Bowers struggled with injuries but still TE1 in dynasty. Tre Tucker showed flashes of being a contributor on offense. Geno Smith was a WALKING TRASH CAN in this offense. One of the worst offensive lines of all time. Ashton Jeanty struggled behind terrible offensive line. Looked good on some opportunities and still have high hope for top 5 RB. Jach Bech was a massive rookie disapointment and Dont'e Thornton Jr. MAY not be able to play football (Trash).

MIA.name: Miami Dolphins
MIA.notes_2026: Dolphins sign Malik Willis in free agency to 3 year deal. Mobile QB with high fantasy upside. Offense skill positions (other than Achance) now worst in NFL. Unsure who starting WR will be. Dolphins drafted Caleb Douglas & Chris Bell (both have very good potential). Malik Washington is not good, Tutu Atwell unlikely to be highly productive, Terrace Marshall & Turpin also signed. Both are also not good. Greg Dulcich does have potential if Willis can get the offense working

historical_2025.notes: Tua looked absolutely AWFUL at QB eventually being benched for Ewers. Tyreek Hill blew up knee looking at 12+ month recovery. Achane absolutely ELITE RB. Jaylen Waddle operated as teams WR1 once Tyreek Hill went down. Malik Washington is not good but had production due to volume. Darren Waller (old) was productive but hurt all the time. Greg Dulcich was productive TE.

MIN.name: Minnesota Vikings
MIN.notes_2026: Jefferson dynasty cornerstone regardless of QB. Vikings sign Kyler Murray from Cardinals who is looking to turn around career. Kyler will compete with McCarthy for QB1 spot but JJ Nine McCarthy is BOOTY CHEEKS, so Kyler will start & could elevate offense a lot. Jefferson long term value there regardless of QB, Addison is great WR but volume still isnt there, interesting buy if can get him for cheap. Aaron Jones may phase out and took a pay cut to stay. Mason is mid. Rookie RB Claiborne COULD build out a role if he can beat out Mason. Hockenson should be better than last year but his play has declined a lot.

historical_2025.notes: JJ Nine McCarthy turned out to be one of the worst QBs to ever play football. The offense literally could't do anything with him at QB. Wentz was not much better. Max Brosmer was straight BUTT. Jefferson & Addison struggled to produce but that was it was out of their control. TJ Hockenson struggled and did not look good. Nailor was a productive WR3 & showed a lot of promise. Disaster season. Jones could not stay healthy & regressed while Jordan Mason was inneficient with increased opportunity.

NE.name: New England Patriots
NE.notes_2026: Maye dynasty franchise QB. Patriots signed Romeo Doubs from Packers (huge potential and solid player could be WR2). The Patriots WILL trade for AJ Brown from Eagles & he will be their WR1 next year. He has MASSIVE potential with Drake Maye. Henderson should operate as lead back in 2026, but Vrabel has done strange things with playing time for backs & Stevenson will work in. Henry is still TE1, but drafted Raridon round 3 TE — Vrabel uses TEs.

historical_2025.notes: Maye full season — top dynasty QB target. Stefon Diggs emerged as WR1 & had highly productive season. Hunter Henry Drake Maye favorite TE and was inconsistent, but productive. Boutte showed flashes of a breakout but it never came. Mack Hollins was productive but didnt hold value. Henderson did not play much & struggled first half of season but eventually overtook Stevenson as RB1. Stevenson still played a major part in the offense despite worse play. Drake Maye emerged as #1 dynasty asset potential with ELITE upside & consistency.

NO.name: New Orleans Saints
NO.notes_2026: Tyson 1.08 pick — immediate target share in rebuilding offense. Significantly hurts Olave potential, but Olave is highly talented WR. Shough surrounded with Weapons in New Orleans by signing Travis Etienne Jr. in free agency (lots of potential to be high producer). Alvin Kamara value is toast. Interested to see how Olave & Tyson split target share & if the rest of the offense other than Etienne holds fantasy value.

historical_2025.notes: Carr retired. Rattler struggled early & replaced by Shough who BALLED OUT. Shaheed traded to Seahawks mid season. Full rebuild mode. Kamara washed, hurt & not productive. Kendre Miller showed promise but got hurt Devin Neal & Audric Estime filled in. Chris Olave was hihgly productive. Amazing year and fully healthy (super slept on). Vele showed promise for potential breakout.

NYG.name: New York Giants
NYG.notes_2026: Giants fired Daboll & brought in Harbaugh. Harbaugh should revamp offense. Brought it Isaiah Likely from Baltimore who seriously hurts Theo Johnson dynasty value (likely should be good). NY did not draft RB & Skattebo will be the starter week 1 of 2026 unless they add someone. He could have a monster season with his Jaxson Dart connection growing. Jaxson Dart gets to play with Malik Nabers for the first time & connection should be electric. Darnell Mooney is a great signing & is on a 1 year prove it deal in NY. If Dart play continues, Mooney will have value.

historical_2025.notes: Wilson starts the season & benched for 1st round rookie Jaxon Dart. Dart absolutely electric start to rookie season posting GREAT fantasy numbers. Cam Skattebo highly productive RB, but injured. Malik Nabers tore ACL week 2. Wan'Dale Robinson emerged as WR1 & completely broke out. Tracey value is out the window, won't be highly productive player. Filled in fine, but not the future in NY. Slayton does not have dynasty value.

NYJ.name: New York Jets
NYJ.notes_2026: Fields traded. Jets bring in Geno Smith from Raiders who was AWFUL in Vegas last year. Could provide decent opportunity for Garrett Wilson (elite WR needs better QB play). Adonai Mitchell likely to play WR3 behind Omar Cooper Jr (round 1 pick). Will have to monitor closely to see. Drafted Kenyon Sadiq Round 1 TE (high ceiling but may take time to break out). Mason Taylor will likely be TE2 behind Sadiq but could be starter at beginning of season. Breece Hall playing on franchise tag. The talent is there, but the offense has to be able to score for Breece to be productive.

historical_2025.notes: Fields disaster. Brutal season. Wilson hurt most of season. While ELITE WR, cannot produce with HORRIBLE QB play. Hall failed to meet expectations again with Braelon Allen & Isaiah Davis eating into carries. Mitchell had flashes as a WR2, but lacked consistency. John Metchie III played decent. Mason Taylor played very well.

PHI.name: Philadelphia Eagles
PHI.notes_2026: AJ Brown is going to be traded to the Patriots. He will not be on the team. Devonta Smith is primed to BREAKOUT as a WR1 IF he can maintain target share. Drafted Makai Lemon Round 1, Eli Stowers (great receiving TE who was also scouted as WR). Eagles also traded for Dontayvion Wicks from Packers who is a talented WR but hasn't put it all together yet. Signed Marquiese Brown in free agency who is expected to operate as WR3/4. There are a lot of mouths to feed on this offense and not all of them can be fantasy relevant. Saquon had a rough 2025 season but should be great in 2026 IF Jalen Hurts can improve his level of play. There are a lot of targets available with AJ Brown leaving.

historical_2025.notes: Saquon dynasty sell high if possible. AJ Brown Trade Rumors & Inconsistency. Goedert scored tons of TD & was productive if scored. Smith ascending. Hurts struggled but produced well in fantasy.

PIT.name: Pittsburgh Steelers
PIT.notes_2026: Bernard round 2 — Steelers traded up. Physical WR2. Steelers traded for Michael Pittman Jr. who is aging but will elevate the passing game. DK Metcalf looks to get back on track in 2026 & establish himself as WR1. Crowded WR room. Steelers drafted WR Riley Nowakowski in the 4th round & he will likely operate as WR5 or return specialist. Signed Rico Dowdle to 2 year deal in free agency. Warren is slotted to be 1A and Dowdle 1B, but any injury to either back makes the other a home run hit in fantasy for 2026. Aaron Rodgers SHOULD return to Steelers, but also drafted Drew Allar (bad QB but good prospect who should be rostered because he can progress)

historical_2025.notes: Pickens traded to Cowboys & did not play for Steelers in 2025. Steelers traded for DK Metcalf & he had dissapointing season. Opportunity was there but DK struggled to be a weekly WR2. Still potential in Metcalf. Calvin Austin, Roman Wilson, and Thielen were irrelevant for fantasy. Pat Freiermuth had an up and down year, struggling to be fully utilized with Jonnu Smith & Darnell Washington also seeing lots of playing time. Jaylen Warren was a highly productive back & so was Kenneth Gainwell. Both backs had fantastic seasons & were viable in fantasy. Kenneth Gainwell even was playable as flex/RB2.

SEA.name: Seattle Seahawks
SEA.notes_2026: Price round 1 RB — bell cow opportunity. with Charbonnet recovering from torn ACL. Lose OC Kubiak to Raiders. JSN top dynasty asset (ELITE). Shaheed could see expanded role in 2026 with full offseason with team. Charbonnet has never truly broken out & torn ACL places concern on productivity when back from injury. Cooper Kupp is dust & totally cooked. Tory Horton could be a break out candidate in 2026. Darnold needs to be rostered in all formats & decent low end QB1 with a high powered offense.

historical_2025.notes: JSN WR1 breakout, top dynasty asset. Sam Darnold fantastic season, low end QB1 option weekly. No mobility so lower floor. Charbonet tore ACL & Kenneth Walker III finished superbowl MVP. DK Metcalf traded & Lockett didnt play in Seattle in 2025. Traded for Shaheed productive deep threat with high ceiling but very low floor. Kupp WAY BEYOND prime. Retiring soon. Tory Horton COULD have upside if he sees playing time increase. Does not heavily utilize TE. Barner fine, Arroyo shows long term potential & very athletic.

SF.name: San Francisco 49ers
SF.notes_2026: Stribling round 2 has opportunity to break out early. Mike Evans signing in free agency. Should be alpha WR1 but aging & injury risk. Potential sell high but should produce in 2026.  Wills round 3 TE. Ricky Pearsall has an opportunity to break out as a WR2 but needs to stay healthy enough to do so. Signed Christian Kirk in offseason, veteran with no fantasy value but will take away targets. Kittle recovering from torn achilles. Drafted Wills the TE. Tonges should function as primary TE2, Wills will be TE3. CMC is a potential sell high candidate but know if healthy he is the number 1 overall RB in 2026.

historical_2025.notes: CMC elite performance & played all 17 games. Brock Purdy struggled with injury & Mac Jones filled in proving he can be a starter in the NFL. WR production limited & offense funneled through CMC & Kittle mainly. Jennings high end WR3, Ricky Pearsall struggled to stay healthy all season & did not have a great year. Opportunity to break out later. Kendrick Bourne was productive but inconsistent. Brian Robinson was a back up only. Bandon Aiyuk missed entire season due to ACL tear & relationship with south with 49ers front office. No future with the team. George Kittle torn achilles end of season.

TB.name: Tampa Bay Buccaneers
TB.notes_2026: Emeka Egbuka is primed for a MASSIVE breakout if Baker Mayfield has strong 2026 season. Mike Evans left team in free agency for 49ers, opening up opportunity for Jalen McMillan & Tez Johnson (young & productive WR). Chris Godwin aging & struggles to produce but could have decent low end WR2/WR3 role over next few years. Bucs signed Kenneth Gainwell in free agency who was HIGHLY productive in Pittsburgh. Strong concern for Bucky Irving owners. We will have to see how the RB split carries. Will get a better idea through the summer and camp.

historical_2025.notes: Evans aging & injured. Godwin struggled to stay healthy. Egbuka rookie 1st round WR amazing first half of season but fell off. Jalen McMillan returned from injury and was solid. Tez Johnson rookie WR was electric on limited opportunities. Bucs struggled to run the ball. Bucky Irving had MAJOR sophomore slump that was caused by shoulder injury and stagnant offense. Baker Mayfield looked like MVP candidate but struggled second half of season. Sean Tucker saw major goalline work but nothing else. Rachaad White filled in as lead back when Irving was hurt. Cade Otton productive TE when the ball came his way.

TEN.name: Tennessee Titans
TEN.notes_2026: Titans draft Carnell Tate 1.04. Large potential for early breakout & Cam Ward to deliver at a high level. Calvin Ridley is trash but if Cam Ward can sling the rock could produce (we don't want to own Ridley). Ayomanor & Dike COULD be good if Ward produces but with Tate around now will never be WR1 on the team. Could gain relevance though. Gunnar Helm 4th round TE draft pick & projected starter could have value. Tony Pollard is aging & only has a few years left (not attractive to own). Tyjae Spears cannot stay healthy. Have to image the Titans will invest in another RB long term (Nicholas Singleton is probably not that guy either).

historical_2025.notes: Ward 1.01 franchise QB. Brutal rookie season and little to no fantasy relevance coming out of it. Offense disfunctional. Ridley complete dissapointment & got injured. Elic Ayomanor & Chimere Dike were Cam Ward's favorite targets but were not startable due to poor offense. Tony Pollard was a low end RB2 and high end RB3 some weeks if he scored, but Tyjae Spears ate into his recieving load. Tyjae struggled to stay healthy. Okonkwo was not highly relevant.

WAS.name: Washington Commanders
WAS.notes_2026: Williams round 3 WR could connect early with Daniels. Terry McLaurin is aging, but if he can stay healthy, he will produce over next few years. Signed TE Chig Okonkwo who could 100% produce well with Jayden Daniels but hadn't provded it with Titans yet. Jacory Croskey-Merritt is set to be 1A RB while they sign Bucs RB Rachaad White in free agency. Could function as 1B and recieving back. Very curious to see how this dynamic turns out. Brandon Aiyuk WILL SIGN with Commanders or be traded to them. Unsure if he will be good following knee surgery, but he will be in Washington next Season. Daniels top 5 SF dynasty QB. Buy him.

historical_2025.notes: Daniels srtuggled with injury & so did McLaurin. Disaster season for commanders. Austin Ekeler tore achilles (cooked), & Jacory Croskey-Merritt took over lead back responsibility. Chris Rodriguez Jr functioned as RB2. Deebo Samuel Functioned as WR1 with Terry McLarin only playing 10 games. Marcus Mariota played QB most of season & offense was horrible. Croskey-Merritt did not live up to the hype but the Commanders seem to really like him. Zach Ertz was a productive TE but tore ACL at end of the season. Jaylin Lane & Luke McCaffrey will likely never have real dynasty value. Jayden Daniels WILL BOUNCE BACK & become Elite QB.
